markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Ingest ASR/caption artifacts as passages with timing anchors, media hashes, and provenance. This MT belongs to SourceIngestionAndEvidence. Ingest project, governance, media, and research sources with evidence and failure receipts.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Source ingestion must be allowlisted, hash-based, restartable, and secret-aware.
PDF/media extraction failures must produce receipts and repairable metadata rather than silently indexing partial content.
scope.expected_behavior: MediaTranscriptIngestion leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: MediaTranscriptIngestion is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 4a9aa81d; real-PG tests green (group total 80 passed/0 failed; lopdf 0.41 MIT only dep added). Pending adversarial code review before READY_FOR_VALIDATION.

concerns: NO ASR contract honored: parses operator-provided SRT/VTT/JSON caption artifacts into media_time-anchored cues; never invokes speech recognition. Matches OPERATOR LAW.

Malformed-cue policy is correct: bad timing line / negative range / missing text are recorded as typed MalformedCue and parsing CONTINUES -> 'partial' receipt naming every lost cue + repair entry; a file that is not the claimed format (VTT without WEBVTT header) is a whole-file PARSE_ERROR. No silent success.

Transcript cues DO pass redact_spans (media_transcript secret_scan=true), so a credential spoken/captioned into a transcript is redacted/blocked like any text. Whole-file preflight also runs over the raw artifact text.

Minor: JSON whisper-style start/end are f64 seconds rounded to ms; extreme/NaN/Inf f64 are filtered by the (e>=s && s>=0.0) guard (NaN comparisons are false -> dropped as malformed). No panic on pathological floats.
evasion_probes: well-formed SRT -> 2 cues, correct ms, cue_index CONFIRMED.
VTT missing header -> PARSE_ERROR CONFIRMED; short MM:SS.mmm timestamps + <tag> stripping CONFIRMED.
negative range / non-numeric timing -> MalformedCue, parsing continues, partial CONFIRMED.
whisper segments shape -> ms conversion CONFIRMED; unknown shape ({foo:1}) -> PARSE_ERROR CONFIRMED.
all-malformed artifact -> typed whole-file PARSE_ERROR CONFIRMED.
millisecond field must be exactly 3 digits -> guards timestamp spoofing/overflow CONFIRMED.

evidence: Adversarial code review PASS/PASS_WITH_NOTES (ADVERSARIAL_CODE_REVIEWER-20260611-PM); only LOW non-blocking notes.

wp_validator_review_v1.review_scope: Validated only MTs that were READY_FOR_VALIDATION in WP-KERNEL-009; no ACP used.

findings.summary: MT-088 is READY_FOR_VALIDATION but declares MT-081 as a dependency, and MT-081 is still CLAIMED rather than validator-completed.
findings.required_remediation: Do not resubmit MT-088 until MT-081 is READY_FOR_VALIDATION and passes WP Validator review, or update the dependency graph through the packet authority if the dependency is no longer real.

evidence_basis: Authoritative MT JSON contract and current product tree inspection.
Dependency failures use deterministic packet status/dependency reads only; WIP MT work products were not inspected.
remediation_summary: Do not resubmit MT-088 until MT-081 is READY_FOR_VALIDATION and passes WP Validator review, or update the dependency graph through the packet authority if the dependency is no longer real.

remediation_v2.fail_verdict: FAIL_V1 BLOCKED_ON_DEPENDENCY (MT-081 unfinished at validation time)
remediation_v2.remediation: Dependency resolved: MT-081 is now implemented+hardened+verified (commit 5876feaf) and READY_FOR_VALIDATION. This MT own implementation passed adversarial review; the only V1 blocker was the MT-081 dependency. Resubmitting for V2 re-validation.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-088 requires ingesting operator-provided ASR/caption transcript artifacts as media-time-anchored KnowledgeSpans with provenance, partial receipts on malformed cues, typed whole-file failures, and PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority. The code parses SRT/VTT/JSON into time-coded cues, wires them to persisted MediaTime spans, and proves the full pipeline (receipt + spans + repair enqueue) against real PostgreSQL. It implements the MT and aligns with current Master Spec 2.3.13.11.
findings: transcripts.rs (commit 4a9aa81d, full file): parse_srt/parse_vtt/parse_json_transcript convert SRT (HH:MM:SS,mmm), WebVTT (WEBVTT header required, MM:SS.mmm short stamps, NOTE/STYLE/REGION skipped, tag stripping), and canonical/whisper JSON into TranscriptCue{index,start_ms,end_ms,text}. Malformed cues (bad timing, negative range, missing text) are recorded as typed MalformedCue{line,reason} and parsing CONTINUES (transcripts.rs:198-223, 278-303, 363-377). Zero well-formed cues -> typed PARSE_ERROR; unknown extension -> UNSUPPORTED_FORMAT (transcripts.rs:225-233, 407-412). Correctly NO ASR (parses operator artifacts only), matching the WP constraint.

engine.rs:1123-1160 IngestionSourceKind::MediaTranscript branch maps each cue to SpanAnchor::MediaTime{start_ms,end_ms,cue_index}, sets status Partial + error_class MalformedCue + spans_failed=malformed.len() when lossy, and packs malformed_cues into error_detail. Success when no malformed cues.

engine.rs persist_attempt (420-527) persists real DB rows: record_extraction_receipt (status/error_class/error_detail/spans_failed/spans_produced), replace_source_spans, EventLedger event, source index rollup, and enqueue_repair for repairable Partial/Failed classes. MalformedCue is repairable (receipts.rs:104-105 is_repairable excludes only UnsupportedFormat/SecretBlocked).

Integration test mt088_transcripts_become_time_coded_spans_with_partial_on_malformed_cues (knowledge_ingestion_pipeline_tests.rs:484-559) runs against real PostgreSQL via ingestion_pg() and the real ingest_file_bytes pipeline: asserts SRT -> 2 MediaTime spans (1000/4000/cue_index 0); VTT with one malformed cue -> persisted Partial receipt, error_class MalformedCue, spans_failed=1, malformed_cues array len 1, repair enqueued; whisper JSON -> 500/2250 ms spans; fully malformed -> persisted Failed/ParseError. This is real-resource proof, not mock-only.
No deferred-live escapes in the transcript path (grep todo!/unimplemented!/Unavailable/"deferred"/"pending MT-" over transcripts.rs = no hits).

Spec 2.3.13.11 (master-spec-v02.194/02-system-architecture.md:2694-2709) defines KnowledgeSpan with a media-time range as a citeable evidence unit; line 268 names ASR transcripts as importable knowledge content. Implementation aligns.

Aside (non-blocking, expected): MT contract cites v02.192 spec path and prior FAIL_V1 was a pure dependency-ordering block (MT-081 not yet validated), not a code defect; remediation_v2 records MT-081 now READY/implemented. Neither is a code problem.
evidence_checked: .GOV/spec/master-spec-v02.194/spec-modules/02-system-architecture.md (2.3.13.11 KnowledgeSpan media-time anchor; line 268 ASR transcript)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
